component.name: Monitor Layout
component.summary: Arrange displays and mirror groups on GNOME

description.p: Monitor Layout is a visual display manager for GNOME on Wayland. It talks directly to Mutter's DisplayConfig D-Bus interface — the same mechanism GNOME Settings uses — and adds capabilities the built-in panel does not offer, most importantly partial mirroring: two displays can form one mirror group while a third remains an independent extended display. This is ideal for KVM-over-IP setups where a remote console should mirror the main monitor.

ul.li: Drag-and-drop layout editor with edge snapping and live validation
Partial mirroring: mirror any subset of displays, keep the rest extended
Resolution, refresh rate, scaling (including fractional), rotation, primary display
Named profiles matched by stable EDID identity, not connector names
Safe applies: GNOME's standard keep-or-revert confirmation, plus a crash-proof rollback watchdog for previews
KVM display detection with user-editable friendly names

description.p: Initial release: visual layout editor, partial mirroring, profiles, safe apply and rollback.
